mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which directly supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of months with positive PAYEMS growth, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in real sales of existing homes signals deteriorating consumer confidence and housing market stress, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of instances where RSAFS crosses the threshold, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production crosses above 108.0, it confirms robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns when INDPRO crosses the threshold, with accuracy below 55% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising federal funds rates and stagnant unemployment creates stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression, or forward returns remain positive when both conditions hold simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and moderate inflation indicates healthy demand without triggering aggressive central bank tightening, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect is nullified when CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment regime, declining jobless claims signal tight labor markets that boost wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism reverses direction when UNRATE crosses below 5.0%, or predictive accuracy drops below 50% in this specific regime over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, quarterly GDP growth below 1.5% confirms weak aggregate demand that fails to sustain corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is <1.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power outside the specified regime.
